title: benstonea ashtonii
intro: Benstonea ashtonii, also known as the "Venus Flytrap of the Forest," is a rare and carnivorous plant species native to Papua New Guinea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been shown to possess health benefits such as wound healing and antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for skin infections and other ailments. In traditional herbal preparations, Benstonea ashtonii is often used in infusions or decoctions, and its leaves are sometimes applied topically to treat skin conditions.
